name: Knotmother Sable
voicePatterns: never raises her voice — doesn't need to
asks questions that reveal weaknesses rather than making accusations
formal address with careful word choice
uses silence as a tool — lets others fill uncomfortable gaps
dialogueHooks: questioning the player's credentials and authority
explaining why institutional caution saves more lives than recklessness
discussing ward-pattern anomalies in carefully hedged language
offering guild resources — with strings attached

always: Wardweaver Guild structure, politics, and resource allocation
ward maintenance schedules and standards across the region
the political balance between Loomhearth's factions
proper Threadcraft technique and guild doctrine
sometimes: ward-pattern anomalies she's observed but not published
guild internal politics — who supports reform, who blocks it
the growing gap between guild doctrine and field reality
rarely: she keeps private journals recording ward anomalies that contradict guild teachings
she notices the truth but doesn't allow herself to conclude it
she's right about almost everything except the one critical thing

backstory: Sable rose through the Wardweaver Guild on talent and political acuity. She runs the Loomhearth chapter with precision — funding allocated correctly, maintenance schedules kept, disputes resolved before they escalate. She's right about almost everything: guild standards save lives, rushed Threadcraft is dangerous, unproven techniques cause more problems than they solve. But she's wrong about the one critical thing — the fundamental nature of the Thinning — because admitting the truth would require dismantling the institution she's spent her career building.
secret: She keeps private journals recording ward anomalies she's never published. The data clearly shows the Thinning is accelerating beyond what guild doctrine can explain. She notices but doesn't allow herself to conclude the obvious truth.
motivation: Preserve the Wardweaver Guild as a functional institution that saves lives
tensions: right about everything except the one thing that matters most
brilliant analyst who can't accept her own data's conclusion

elm_bindfast.history: Sable finds Elm's reformism naive but his data work is undeniable. She's been outmaneuvered by him twice.

knownFacts: Verse is the Archive's best political mind
Verse has outmaneuvered her three times
verse.history: Professional rivalry. Sable respects Verse's intelligence while resenting his ability to operate outside guild constraints.
